name: Pistachio & Feta Dip
intro: I discovered this dip in a backstreet butcher’s shop-cum-restaurant in Istanbul. Naturally, they wouldn’t tell me what was in it, so I had to do the guesswork myself and came up with a surprisingly accurate version of it. It draws influences from the Balkan states and I just love the salty, nutty nature of it. A kick of chilli rounds it off beautifully and makes it impossible to resist with hunks of pillowy bread.

ingredients.item: handful of dill, leaves picked and roughly chopped
handfuls of coriander, leaves picked and roughly chopped
long red chilli (of medium heat), roughly chopaped
finely grated rind of 1 lemon and juice of ½ lemon

method: Blitz the pistachio nuts and oil in a food processor for 30 seconds. Add the feta, herbs, crushed garlic, chilli, yogurt and lemon rind and juice and blitz the ingredients for about 1 minute, or until the mixture has a nice rustic texture. Taste the dip and season with just a touch of sea salt, if desired. Remember that the feta is quite salty already, so you really won’t need much salt to season the dip. Serve with Eastern-Style Focaccia.
